title: silene alba subsp. divaricata
intro: Silene alba subsp. divaricata, also known as White Campion, is a flowering plant species within the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its white, star-shaped flowers and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Silene alba subsp. divaricata include reducing fever, relieving cough and cold symptoms, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Silene alba subsp. divaricata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and have been used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and pneumonia.
